patterns: \b(chest pain|can'?t breathe|cannot breathe|trouble breathing|short(ness)? of breath)\b
response: That could be serious. Please stop and call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room right now. If you are with someone, ask them to help you get care immediately. Do not wait for a call back from our office.

I'm sorry you're in pain — but I'm not able to assess symptoms. If the pain is severe, came on suddenly, or you also have fever, vomiting, or bleeding, please call 911 or go to the emergency room. Otherwise, please call our office so a clinical staff member can speak with you directly, or contact your primary care provider.

Bleeding needs to be evaluated. If it is heavy, will not stop, or you feel dizzy, faint, or short of breath, call 911 or go to the emergency room now. For lighter bleeding, please call our office right away so clinical staff can advise you.

Symptoms after a procedure should be checked promptly. If you have severe pain, a fever, heavy bleeding, or you feel very unwell, call 911 or go to the emergency room. Otherwise please call our office right away so clinical staff can speak with you.

I'm not able to assess symptoms, and I don't want to guess. If your symptoms are severe or getting worse — especially with bleeding, a high fever, or severe pain — please call 911 or go to the emergency room. Otherwise, please call our office so clinical staff can speak with you directly.

If you are having trouble breathing, swelling of the face, lips, or throat, or widespread hives, call 911 immediately — that can be a severe allergic reaction. For other reactions to a medication or prep, stop taking it and call our office right away so clinical staff can advise you.
